title: Contaminant Source Identification
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Contaminant Source Identification: Contaminant source identification: locate unknown pollution source and quantify emission history from sparse observations. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.transport.adjoint_source…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; transport_model_uncertainty, background_contamination set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: c(x,t) = integral G(x,t; x_s, t_s) * Q(x_s, t_s) dV dt_s (Green's function convolution)
E.world_state_x: source location (x_s, y_s, z_s) and emission rate history Q(t_s)
E.observation_y: contaminant concentration measurements c_i(x_i, t_i) at N_sensors locations

W.regime: Existence of the recovered source_location_strength_time is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); transport_model_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: source location within 100 m for 10+ sensors in 1 km domain. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |transport_model_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_MCMC_samples * N_sensors * N_timesteps) for Bayesian inversion per iteration
